title: Bridge Essay: History of winning remodels thalamo-PFC circuit to reinforce social dominance.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Wind/Pride** (Phase 4) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Wind/Pride to mechanistic neuroscience.

**History of winning remodels thalamo-PFC circuit to reinforce social dominance.** — Science. PMID: 28706064. DOI: 10.1126/science.aak9726. Mental strength and history of winning play an important role in the determination of social dominance. However, the neural circuits mediating these intrinsic and extrinsic factors have remained unclear. Working in mice, we identified adorsomedial prefrontal cortex (dmPFC) neural population showing "effort"-related firing during moment-to-moment competition in the dominance tube test. Activation or inhibition of the dmPFC induces instant winning or losing, respectively. In vivo optogenetic-base
